rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant A.H. Voss Company's Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiff Gordon Andersen's Claim for Punitive Damages is GRANTED. Defendant sustained its burden of showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence that Defendant acted with intent, malice, or oppression. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855). Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's special interrogatories are factually devoid as it contains boilerplate answers that restate Plaintiff's allegations and laundry lists of people and/or documents. (Defendant's Ex. E [Plaintiff's response to special interrogatory no. 23].) Plaintiff's objection to Plaintiff's Responses to A.H. Voss Company's Special Interrogatories is overruled. The Court exercises its discretion to consider the verifications to Plaintiff's interrogatory responses, which were executed by Plaintiff on July 12, 2018. (Passalacqua Supplemental Declaration at Ex. A; Jacobs v. Coldwell Banker Residential Brokerage Co. (2017) 14 Cal. App. 5th 438, 449.) Plaintiff failed to submit sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. The depositions of Stephen Eckstrom and declaration of Richard Cohen, M.D., M.P.H. are not sufficient to create a triable issue as they do not demonstrate a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. (Plaintiff's Ex. C) While Plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find Plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017, 1049; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48, 60-61.) Hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/CML) (4:26 Late) = (503/CML)

Defendant Golden Gate Drywall's Motion for Summary Adjudication as to Plaintiff Gordon Andersen's Claim for Punitive Damages is GRANTED. Defendant sustained its burden of showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence that Defendant acted with intent, malice, or oppression. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855). Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's special interrogatories are factually devoid as it contains boilerplate answers that restate Plaintiff's allegations and laundry lists of people and/or documents. (Defendant's Ex. E [Plaintiff's responses to special interrogatory nos. 22-24].) Plaintiff failed to submit sufficient evidence to create a triable issue whether Defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. The depositions of Stephen Eckstrom and Matthew Thompson, and declaration of Richard Cohen, M.D., M.P.H. are not sufficient to create a triable issue as they do not demonstrate a willful and conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. (Plaintiff's Exs. B and C) While Plaintiff does not need to prove his case for punitive damages at the summary adjudication stage, the Court takes the higher proof standard into consideration and concludes that no reasonable jury could find Plaintiff's evidence to be clear and convincing proof of malice, fraud or oppression. (See American Airlines, Inc. v. Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1017, 1049; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48, 60-61.) Hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/CML) (4:25 Late)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. DEFENDANT FISHER CONTROLS INTERNATIONAL LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML) (Late 4:27)

rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ; Notice Of Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 6, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant General Cable Corporation's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED in its entirety. The Court did not consider Defendant's reply papers for failure to deliver courtesy copies. (Local Rule 2.7B.) Defendant sustained its burden of showing that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain needed evidence related to Plaintiff's false representation, intentional tort, and punitive damages claims. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 855). Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's special interrogatories related to false representation, intentional tort, and punitive damages are factually devoid as it contains boilerplate answers that restate Plaintiff's allegations and laundry lists of people and/or documents. (Defendant's Ex. D [Plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories nos. 29-37].) The deposition of Carlos Katz creates a triable issue whether Defendant knew of the hazards of asbestos and failed to include warnings on its products. (Plaintiff's Exhibit B) Hearing will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/CML) (4:29 Late)
